title: My mothers Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far
display_title: My mothers Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far
wikitext:
[]: My mother's Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Wed Sep 14, 2005 12:05 am I will now post quotes from my mother on Evangelion, thus far: Shinj: "God, no wonder he's screwed up, dealing with a bunch of abusive people and that drunken whore(Misato)...he should really just see a therapist and stop listening to what that dickhead(Gendo) says..." Rei: "Wow, she's quiet. I wish I had a daughter like her." Asuka: "She's very motherly and protective of Shinji." Misato: "What a crazy bitch, always yelling at the poor boy. You can't just yank somebody off the street and expect them to pilot big robots! She should get slapped." Gendo: "He's a real asshole, he never spoke to his son in all this time and now he drags him out of his life to pilot the Eva's?!" Evas: "They're robots, right?" Eva Hentai: "So people like watching robots fuck?" I love watching Eva with my mom. It's like watching Mystery Anime Theater 3000, but with just one woman.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 14, 2005 10:40 am My parents have never watched Eva. Which is surprising to me. I've been into it for nearly six years, and they know all about my work on the site, at conventions, and my research project, but they never once asked to see it to figure out what the deal was.

[]: Re: My mother's Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Wed Sep 14, 2005 12:57 pm Takumi Fujiwara wrote: I will now post quotes from my mother on Evangelion, thus far: Shinj: "God, no wonder he's screwed up, dealing with a bunch of abusive people and that drunken whore(Misato)...he should really just see a therapist and stop listening to what that dickhead(Gendo) says..." Rei: "Wow, she's quiet. I wish I had a daughter like her." Asuka: "She's very motherly and protective of Shinji." Misato: "What a crazy bitch, always yelling at the poor boy. You can't just yank somebody off the street and expect them to pilot big robots! She should get slapped."

[]: Re: My mother's Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Sep 14, 2005 1:26 pm Carl Horn wrote: Takumi Fujiwara wrote: I will now post quotes from my mother on Evangelion, thus far: Shinj: "God, no wonder he's screwed up, dealing with a bunch of abusive people and that drunken whore(Misato)...he should really just see a therapist and stop listening to what that dickhead(Gendo) says..." Rei: "Wow, she's quiet. I wish I had a daughter like her." Asuka: "She's very motherly and protective of Shinji." Misato: "What a crazy bitch, always yelling at the poor boy. You can't just yank somebody off the street and expect them to pilot big robots! She should get slapped."

[]: Re: My mother's Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Wed Sep 14, 2005 2:05 pm Digitalex wrote: The Rei comment was amusing though.

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Wed Sep 14, 2005 2:11 pm I think she has Misato and Asuka switched around? Asuka motherly to Shinji? That's an unbelievable idea, shows your mom has a unique perspective and insight :)

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Sep 14, 2005 2:41 pm My mothers only comment was "it's all double-dutch" and my dad thinks I'm crazy because I watch the sub rather than the dub, even though he admits that the dub sounds terrible. :?

[]:             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Wed Sep 14, 2005 2:44 pm K2Grey wrote: I think she has Misato and Asuka switched around?

[]:             Quote Post by Hiromakuta » Wed Sep 14, 2005 5:25 pm Takumi Fujiwara wrote: K2Grey wrote: I think she has Misato and Asuka switched around?

[]:             Quote Post by Magami no D00M » Wed Sep 14, 2005 5:33 pm She seems to root more for the children in this case. Cool.

[]: Re: My mother's Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Sep 14, 2005 11:54 pm Takumi Fujiwara wrote: Digitalex wrote: The Rei comment was amusing though.

[]: Re: My mother's Analysis of Evangelion Thus Far             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Thu Sep 15, 2005 12:11 am Digitalex wrote: Takumi Fujiwara wrote: Digitalex wrote: The Rei comment was amusing though.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Thu Sep 15, 2005 2:32 pm I dont show my mother Eva. But ,from what I have told her, she thinks Hideaki Anno is a Neo-nazi, and that only nazi's watch the show. She thinks Asuka is making ME mentally disturbed and that Shinji's really creepy. She really hasnt said much about Kaji, but she's made weird comments about my Eva action figures. The last anime I made my mom watch was Gravitation (WHY?!!WHAT WAS I THINKING?) concidering I've been into it for about 4 years now. She watched the first 3 episodes and told me they were all abusivea nd they all look underaged. I'd get the same reaction for Eva, 'cept it be more along the lines of, "CHILD PORNOGRAPHY, AND POST TRAUMATIC STRESS DISORDER. ARIEL, IS YOUR FATHER HANGING AROUND?" >_> Luckily, after forcing her to watch the Gravitation series, she has no desire to watch anime with me anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Sep 15, 2005 6:27 pm The Asuka comment makes a lot of sense to me, actually, especially being that it came from a mother. The lot of you may not have a mother like Asuka, but they're most definitely out there.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei_Unit00 » Tue Oct 04, 2005 5:13 pm I haven't shown my mom Evangelion, well, I don't show her any anime. I would hate to see her reaction to Fooly Cooly.

[]:             Quote Post by geishaboy » Tue Oct 04, 2005 10:24 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: The Asuka comment makes a lot of sense to me, actually, especially being that it came from a mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele_05 » Wed Oct 12, 2005 1:20 pm i showed my mom eva episode 19, she freaked out, saying it was the "most satanical, gore-adiction-inducing anime of all time" maybe thats why i hide End of Eva in the darkest corner of my room.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Wed Oct 12, 2005 1:59 pm Actually, I'd have to agree about the Asuka comment. I mean, atleast in the sense that she is protective of Shinji in a way, but not the normal way... If you know what I mean. And I love my parents... That is the exact reason I haven't shown them Evangelion and why I may never show them... Ever. Actually, I really have absolutely no urge to show Evangelion to my parents. So I really never thought about how my parents would react. My dad wouldn't watch it... If he did I'd feel awkward thinking that he'd hate it. My mom well, no... Showing it to her is out of the question. Besides, they're not big anime fans at all. I watched two episodes of DBZ with my dad... It was, of course, the two episodes that Piccolo and Frieza talked to each other right before their fight.

[]:             Quote Post by Shwiggie » Wed Oct 12, 2005 2:57 pm Heh...this reminds me of the time I asked my mother to get something out of my room while I was away at college, and the next time I came home she asked if I was getting into the occult or something. I laughed and said no...she had seen my Death/Rebirth movie book, saw the Japanese text inside with the weird cross symbols and recognized "evangelion" from Bible study at church and got to wondering. Then I told her that it made use of all the BS occult crap that's accumulated around Judaism and Christianity over the years as a background to some of the story, which made her feel better in that she hadn't been that far off :) As for introducing to anime, the only ones in my family aside from my nephews (who watch it on their own) who might be receptive would be my sister-in-law.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 12, 2005 3:41 pm I'm feeling quietly amused by this thread; being that I know I'm old enough to be the parent of many of the regulars here. And I wouldn't show NGE to my parents - even my Dad, who's into things like Star Wars and Dr Who, because he wouldn't take it seriously. And definitely not EoE, which falls squarely into the "Parental Guidance Advised" category i.e. that sort of stuff you are well advised to guide your parents away from.
